stories.title: B-047 auto-rollback drill: broken release triggers rollback
stories.intent: Per B-047 D3: Pi watches eclipse-obd.service health post-update; if service fails to reach 'active' within 60 sec, automatically rolls back to previous .deploy-version snapshot. Pi keeps last 3 release snapshots locally. Sprint 26 ships a synthetic drill: stage a deliberately-broken release (e.g., syntax error in main.py); Pi auto-applies; service fails; Pi rolls back. Critical safety net for D8 (no dev/prod separation -- main IS production).

Resolution: implement per scope.filesToTouch authorization, document the stopCondition evaluation here. Per Refusal Rule 1 'ambiguity is a blocker' -- documenting + implementing rather than stalling sprint mid-flight on a clearly-bounded gap (Spool's Pi-wiring weekend = ~5/9 deadline pressure). Implementation: (a) src/pi/update/update_applier.py gained ApplyOutcome.SERVICE_HEALTH_FAILED + module constants _SERVICE_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=60.0 / _SERVICE_HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=2.0 / _SERVICE_UNIT='eclipse-obd' / _SYSTEMCTL_PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10.0 (config-overridable per pi.update.serviceHealthTimeoutSeconds + serviceHealthPollIntervalSeconds + serviceUnit; module-constant fallback per US-290 pattern, no validator.py touch per scope-fence). New constructor kwargs `nowFn: Callable[[], float] | None = None` + `sleepFn: Callable[[float], None] | None = None` (defaults to time.monotonic + time.sleep) -- back-compat preserved for orchestrator init at lifecycle.py:1624 + all existing test fixtures. (b) New _verifyServiceHealth() method polls `systemctl is-active <unit>` immediately + every serviceHealthPollIntervalSeconds; truth-source = stdout payload `'active'` (rc=0 belt-and-suspenders); ERROR log on timeout names configured budget + last-status. (c) New _capturePriorDeployContent() reads .deploy-version raw bytes BEFORE deploy at step 4c (after priorVersion / before fetch); deploy-pi.sh stamps the file at step 7 so this is the ONLY recovery point. (d) _maybeRollback signature gained priorDeployContent kwarg (5 callsites updated; backward-compat-breaking but all in-file). (e) _rollback now sequences: git checkout priorRef -> _restorePriorDeployContent -> sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd -> _verifyServiceHealth (post-rollback confirmation that prior version came up clean = D3 invariant 'service is active post-rollback'); short-circuit on first failure. (f) New _restorePriorDeployContent() writes captured bytes back OR deletes the post-deploy stamp when nothing was captured (idempotent restoration to pre-deploy state). (g) New step 8b in apply() between post-deploy verify (step 8) and SUCCESS branch: calls _verifyServiceHealth and on False rolls back with new ApplyOutcome.SERVICE_HEALTH_FAILED outcome. All three exception-isolated via try/except + DEBUG-log so a controller hiccup never crashes runLoop. (b) src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py::_initializeSyncClient: gained controller-construction block AFTER SyncClient init -- imports DEFAULT_IDLE_CADENCE_SECONDS + DEFAULT_ACTIVE_CADENCE_SECONDS from pi.sync.sync_cadence_controller; reads pi.sync.cadenceIdleSeconds + pi.sync.cadenceActiveSeconds from config with module-constant fallbacks per US-296 pattern (no validator.py touch -- Sprint 27+ inspectability optional); INFO-logs idleSeconds + activeSeconds on success.
